Skip to main content

Welcome to the Princeton Office

Tips for navigating the RWJF facility in New Jersey

Know Before You Go

We are pleased to welcome visitors to the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation (RWJF) headquarters in Princeton, N.J. Our workplaces are designed to support collaboration, inclusion, and accessibility. To help ensure a smooth and welcoming visit, please review the information below before arriving onsite.

Hours of Operation

Monday to Thursday, 8:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m.

Getting Connected

Visitor Wi-Fi

Visitors can connect to the internet using our Wi-Fi-network.

The Wi-Fi network password changes periodically. The current password will be provided by your host and can be found on signage throughout the facility.

Visitor Registration and Entry Requirements

  • All visitors must be registered by their RWJF host prior to arrival at any RWJF workplace.
  • RWJF hosts are responsible for submitting visitor registration information at least 24 hours or one full business day in advance to Foundation Services for further registration.
  • Visitors actively partnering with RWJF and/or contractors conducting business aligned with RWJF are eligible for workspace reservations at available facilities.
  • Visitors must be greeted by their RWJF host and remain accompanied for the duration of their visit.
  • RWJF hosts are required to remain on-site for the entire duration of their visitors’ time in the facility and are responsible for maintaining awareness of their visitors’ location.

Health and Wellbeing Expectations

  • By entering an RWJF facility, you acknowledge that you are not currently ill.
  • If you have recently been sick, you may enter once you have been fever-free for at least 24 hours and symptoms are improving.
  • If you have been exposed to someone with COVID 19, flu, or another respiratory illness, please monitor symptoms and do not enter the facility if you have a fever.
  • Masks are optional. Masks and other personal protective equipment (PPE), including hand sanitizers and tissues, are available throughout the facility.

Accessibility and Accomodations

RWJF is committed to building an inclusive and accessible workplace.

  • Our facilities are accessible and include:
    • Touch‑free restroom entry and exit
    • Elevators providing access to all floors
  • If you require different or additional accommodations, please contact Accommodation Requests. Please make all requests two (2) weeks prior to the event date, though Human Resources will do their best to address any requests made after that point.

All requests for accommodations will be facilitated by Human Resources and kept confidential, in accordance with RWJF policies.

Eligibility for Workspace and Collaboration Spaces

  • Visitors may not reserve workspaces or meeting rooms directly and must coordinate all space requests through their RWJF host.
  • Facility amenities such as the Fitness Center and other fitness equipment located throughout the facility are not available to visitors.
  • Grantees or partnering organizations who wish to conduct a meeting at one of the Foundation’s three facilities should work with their RWJF contact to coordinate use of our office spaces.
    • A meeting intake form must be submitted by the host at least three (3) weeks prior to the requested event date.
    • Space is not guaranteed until approval is confirmed.

Lactation, Prayer, Meditation, and First Aid Spaces

The following spaces are available to support visitor wellbeing.

  • Lactation Room—equipped with a comfortable recliner, pump, and essential supplies. Includes a sink with disposable pump supplies and cleaning materials.
  • Prayer Room—a serene sanctuary for prayer, meditation, or quiet reflection.
  • Meditation Room—designed for mindfulness and relaxation with guided exercises, cozy seating, ambient lighting, and tranquil sand gardens.
  • First Aid Room—provides essential first aid supplies along with a recliner for relaxation in a quiet environment.

Please contact your host to request a reservation for these spaces.

Additional Services

Printing Services

  • Visitor printing is available through the Support Center.

Lockers with Vecos Smart Technology

We are now providing upgraded personal storage lockers, powered by VECOS smart-lock technology, giving you secure, flexible storage without assigned keys or codes.

Key Features

  • Touchless, app-based access
  • Ability to reserve lockers on demand
  • Automatic release when no longer in use
  • Enhanced security and privacy for personal items

Lockers are intended for daily use and operate as a shared resource on a first-come basis and are not available for long term assignments

If you have questions, need support, or would like a guided walkthrough, Foundation Services is here to help!

Our Policies

Smoke-Free Campus Policy

The Foundation is committed to providing all employees and visitors with a healthy environment.

  • Smoking and the use of all tobacco products, smokeless tobacco products, and electronic smoking/vaporizing devices (including but not limited to e-cigarettes, with or without nicotine) are prohibited on the Foundation campus at all times.
  • The “Foundation campus” means the Foundation’s facilities and outdoor grounds, including parking garages and decks, visitors’ parking lot, and walking trails.

Fragrance and Scent Sensitivity Policy

Exposure to strong scents and fragranced products can cause people with sensitivities and chronic illnesses to suffer additional symptoms.

To help ensure a comfortable work environment:

  • Visitors should reduce or eliminate wearing strongly scented personal products.
  • “Personal products” include perfume, cologne, aftershave, scented lotions, fragranced hair products, and other similar products.
  • Visitors should minimize the display of fragrant flowers in the office (e.g. lilies, hyacinths, lilacs, etc.).

Anti-Harrassment and Anti-Discrimination Policy

The Foundation prohibits any behavior that constitutes harassment or discrimination based on a legally protected characteristic. This applies to all Foundation employees, persons providing services to the Foundation pursuant to a contract (including but not limited to contractors, consultants, and vendors), visitors, or other persons on Foundation property or business. The Foundation expects each individual to contribute to a professional atmosphere that promotes equal opportunity and nondiscriminatory practices and prohibits retaliation against anyone who in good faith reports or participates in an investigation of harassment or discrimination.
